intent: Review whether workflow failure analysis needs a new governed capability or can be handled by existing workflow, evaluation, and Hermes advisory surfaces.
desiredOutcome: A recommendation-only finding that maps the task to existing primitives and identifies whether a capability proposal is needed.

requiredCapabilities.selectionRationale: Workflow failure review begins by inspecting existing workflow contracts and failure/evaluation surfaces.
The question depends on whether existing evaluation gates and benchmark records already cover the need.
Hermes132 may produce bounded source-linked synthesis, but cannot execute or mutate.

uncertainties.summary: It is not yet proven that existing workflow and evaluation capabilities cover all workflow failure analysis needs.
uncertainties.recommendedAction: Run a bounded hermes132 advisory synthesis before proposing a new capability.

uncertainties.summary: Any new capability affecting workflow failure handling may become structural if it changes workflow authority or runtime behavior.
uncertainties.recommendedAction: Keep the first output recommendation-only and route structural changes through proposal and steward review.

evaluationRequirements.notes: Hermes132 pass must name exact initiative and workflow sources.
Audit unit must be bounded to capability gap review.
Hermes132 must emit one session record and one synthesis artifact.
Any capability proposal must be reviewed before promotion.

shortTerm.notes: Use only the bounded source manifest and audit unit for the advisory pass.

working.notes: Project reviewed recommendation to heap only after operator review.

execution.notes: No runtime execution occurs in the Hermes132 advisory route.

institutional.notes: Promote only if the steward accepts the recommendation as durable evidence.

promotionPath.notes: A surviving capability gap should become a proposal or governed evidence note, not a direct runtime change.
